name: Twinleaf Town

dialogue.text: If you're late, I'm fining you $10 million!

mapdesc: A small town with the fresh scent of new leaves in the air. It feels like a place where adventures start. Your own home and your best friend's house are located here.

name: Sandgem Town

dialogue.text: This world is inhabited by creatures known as Pokémon.

mapdesc: A sandy town that is located right next to a beach. It is redolent with the salty scent of the sea. Prof. Rowan's lab is the town's landmark. He conducts his studies on Pokémon here.

name: Jubilife City

dialogue.text: This is the most modern city in all of Sinnoh!

mapdesc: The most modernized city in the Sinnoh region. It is bustling with people on the go.

name: Oreburgh City

dialogue.text: This is an energetic mining town. Almost everyone works in the coal mine.

mapdesc: A vibrant and energetic mining town that is blessed with a precious natural resource. There is a Gym where mine workers take breaks in between their exhausting work shifts. There is a museum that specializes in exhibits related to the town's coal mine. Huge deposits of coal still sleep underneath the ground. The entrance to the Oreburgh Mine is here.

name: Floaroma Town

dialogue.text: Isn't it lovely here? The whole town smells sweet like flowers and honey.

mapdesc: A town of flower lovers, Floaroma Town is always perfumed with the sweet scent of flowers. A short, floral pathway opens up to a sprawling sea of flowers in bloom. In the center of town is the flower shop called Pick a Peck of Colors.

name: Eterna City

dialogue.text: That statue depicts an ancient Pokémon said to have shaped this land.

mapdesc: An old city that shows fading and almost-forgotten vestiges of ancient history. A Gym is indispensable in any town that has many people coming and going. This city is no exception. The Cycle Shop is located here. There is also a big building with a slightly sinister atmosphere. A statue of an ancient Pokémon is prominently and reverently displayed.

name: Hearthome City

dialogue.text: Ohohoho! You have come to challenge moi?

mapdesc: This friendly city started as a place where people and Pokémon gathered, then grew into a center of commerce. If you care about the looks of your Pokémon, head for the Contest Hall. For toughness, go to the Gym. The entrance to Amity Square is here. The square is a place where Trainers can stroll peacefully with Pokémon. There is a building here that is symbolic of the city's status as a place where different cultures meet. A nice fountain with benches, as well as the Pokémon Fan Club, are among the city's amenities.

name: Solaceon Town

dialogue.text: Leave your Pokémon with us, and we'll raise it with care!

mapdesc: The temperate climate makes this town a relaxed and casual place for people and Pokémon to live in. There is a Pokémon Day Care and a Pokémon Center here. Beyond a path hemmed by stands of trees, one can find the entrance to the Solaceon Ruins.
imageCredit: Bulbapedia (Solaceon Town Day Care Center Pt.png)

name: Veilstone City

dialogue.text: I've been training here forever… but I still don't know what it means to be truly strong.

mapdesc: This city was made by carving out steep, rocky mountains. Its isolation limits its contact with other cities. The city puts effort into providing entertainment. Naturally, one can find a Gym here. Conveniently located right next door to the Game Corner is the Prize Exchange. At the highest point in the city is Team Galactic's headquarters. There are warehouses for storing a variety of goods. There is also a major department store.

name: Pastoria City

dialogue.text: Crash! Crash! Crasher Wake!

mapdesc: This city was originally founded to protect the Great Marsh. It has grown naturally over the years. The Gym is a good example of the abundant water resources available to the city. There is a lookout that affords views of the Great Marsh. The gates to the Safari Zone are also here.

name: Celestic Town

dialogue.text: Our little town has guarded the old stories of Sinnoh for generations.

mapdesc: A tiny town that preserves the history of Sinnoh and the old ways of life. In the center of the town is a small shrine that dates to ancient times.

name: Canalave City

dialogue.text: My boat sails for Iron Island. Hop aboard whenever you like!

mapdesc: A port city that is bisected by a canal. It has a distinctly exotic air of foreign culture. Head to the library if you want a workout for the mind. Head to the Gym if you just want to work out. Large ships can always be seen moored at the town's piers.

name: Snowpoint City

dialogue.text: I'm tough because I know how to focus!

mapdesc: A winter wonderland of a city where stout trees and buildings are blanketed in thick snow. The Gym is at the heart of the city. To the north is the Snowpoint Temple.

name: Sunyshore City

dialogue.text: He shut himself in the lighthouse… Our Gym Leader is bored of battling.

mapdesc: A port city that was built around the bay portion of the cape. It is criss-crossed by elevated walkways. The Gym is located atop a rocky outcropping. Down on the beach is a festive bazaar. The Vista Lighthouse's bright beacon illuminates the Sinnoh region.

name: Pokémon League

dialogue.text: Only Trainers who have defeated all eight Gyms may enter here.

mapdesc: Trainers seeking to become the best arrive here after enduring a long and grueling journey.

name: Fight Area

dialogue.text: Strong Trainers from every region gather on this island!

mapdesc: A tiny port town where Trainers who love battling more than eating gather.

name: Survival Area

dialogue.text: This is a town for real survivalists! The mountain is no joke.

mapdesc: A city where hot-blooded Trainers gather to work out and hone their battling skills.

name: Resort Area

dialogue.text: Ahhh, this is the place where adults come to play.

mapdesc: A city that attracts Trainers who know there are other ways of enjoying Pokémon than battling.

dialogue.text: They say a Pokémon of emotion sleeps at the bottom of this lake.

name: Oreburgh Gate

dialogue.text: Take this! It's Rock Smash! Smash those cracked boulders right out of your way!

mapdesc: Once one gets through the tunnel, the city of Oreburgh is just a stone's throw away.

name: Valley Windworks

dialogue.text: This facility is now under the control of Team Galactic!

mapdesc: Numerous wind turbines stand in the vicinity of the Valley Windworks. Winds blowing through the canyon feed the wind turbines to generate clean electricity.

name: Eterna Forest

dialogue.text: Um… may I go through the forest with you? It's a little scary alone.

mapdesc: A forest enveloped in chilly air. Thick stands of trees turn the forest into a natural maze. Deep in the forest is the Old Chateau. It is falling into disrepair after being abandoned by its owner.

dialogue.text: Mira is lost… Can you take Mira to the exit?

name: Mt. Coronet

dialogue.text: This mountain divides Sinnoh in two. They say the region began here.

mapdesc: A sacred mountain that is capped the year round with snow. A gigantic maze sprawls inside it. There is an ancient ruin named the Spear Pillar located here.

dialogue.text: I'm digging my way to the ruins! Don't rush me — archaeology takes time!

name: Pal Park

dialogue.text: Welcome! Pokémon that migrated from faraway regions live in this preserve.

mapdesc: This used to be the location of the Safari Zone, but is now the Pal Park.

name: Iron Island

dialogue.text: The mine here is abandoned… but it's perfect for training. Shall we team up?

mapdesc: A small, ore-rich island that is off the coast of Eterna City. Inside the island are remnants of an iron ore mining operation from the past.

dialogue.text: The lake… they drained the whole lake! Those Galactic monsters!
Hmph. You're just not strong enough to protect anything.

name: Fuego Ironworks

dialogue.text: We forge parts for Poké Balls here! The floor tiles move — watch your step!

mapdesc: The sprawling ironworks is located with the forest at its back. The ironworks refines iron ore mined from Mt. Coronet to make iron and to manufacture mechanical parts.

dialogue.text: Time and space will bend to me. Here, I will create my new world.
No matter how far you walk… you end up back where you began.

name: Victory Road

dialogue.text: This is the final test. If your team can't cut it, turn back now!

mapdesc: Trainers seeking to become the best arrive here after enduring a long and grueling journey.

name: Fullmoon Island

dialogue.text: On nights of the full moon, a Pokémon here shares pleasant dreams.

mapdesc: A small, crescent-shaped pond that lies inside a closed-off forest.

name: Newmoon Island

dialogue.text: A nightmare that never ends… that is the gift of the one who lives here.

mapdesc: A small, round pond that lies inside a closed-off forest.

name: Stark Mountain

dialogue.text: I'm taking the Magma Stone home as a souvenir! …What? The volcano's rumbling?

mapdesc: A rugged, seemingly indestructible rock mountain that is thickly blanketed by the volcanic ash it spews. Inside the mountain, fissures in spilled and hardened lava form enormous caverns.

name: Flower Paradise

dialogue.text: Flowers as far as the eye can see… They say a gratitude Pokémon plays here.

mapdesc: A speck of an island far from any civilization. It is covered in an abundance of flowers.

name: Route 201

dialogue.text: Wait! Don't go into the tall grass! Wild Pokémon will attack you!

mapdesc: A small path through a lush, green, wooded area. The densely grown trees give off a thick aroma.

name: Route 202

dialogue.text: Here, let me show you how to catch a Pokémon!

mapdesc: A winding path that twists through grassy fields. Young Trainers like to test their battle skills here.

name: Route 203

dialogue.text: You know what? Shorts are comfy and easy to wear!

mapdesc: Despite being so close to a big town, this path retains its relaxed, natural atmosphere. Once one gets through the tunnel, the city of Oreburgh is just a stone's throw away.

name: Route 204

dialogue.text: The path ahead was ravaged by rockslides. You'll need to smash through!

mapdesc: A charming natural path that wanders past many ponds and groves of trees. The Ravaged Path was hewn through a rocky outcropping. It is a shortcut that leads straight to Floaroma Town.

name: Route 205

dialogue.text: Have you seen my papa? He works at the windworks, but the door is locked…

mapdesc: A quietly following stream and hilly terrain with one-way ledges make this a fun area for adventure. Near the entrance of the Eterna Forest is a cabin whose keepers offer rest to weary travelers that stop by. A wide, wooden walkway spans a large pond. Fishermen idle their time away dangling lines from the walkway.

name: Route 206

dialogue.text: This is Cycling Road! No bike, no entry — them's the rules!

mapdesc: Smoothly paved in asphalt, the Cycling Road is heavenly for bicycle lovers. Below the Cycling Road, one can see the entrance to Wayward Cave.

name: Route 207

dialogue.text: This slope's too steep to climb on foot. You'd need a bicycle!

mapdesc: Located at the foot of Mt. Coronet, this road is carved into the rugged, rocky terrain.

name: Route 208

dialogue.text: The Berry Master lives just west of here. He'll share Berries every day!

mapdesc: A lush field of grass spreads from Mt. Coronet's sheer rock face, creating a vista of contrasts.

name: Route 209

dialogue.text: The tower here is where departed Pokémon are laid to rest.

mapdesc: The streams forded by this path wind past copses and grassy patches in a serene and soothing manner. The Lost Tower is where Pokémon are laid to rest when their lives come to an end.

name: Route 210

dialogue.text: Moo! I mean — welcome to Café Cabin! Our Moomoo Milk is fresh!

mapdesc: This narrow route is lined with deep, tall grass that tickles the noses of people straying off the path. The Cafe Cabin stands in a wood. The house special is freshly milked Moomoo Milk. A path made by clearing an overgrown canyon between mountains. It is always shrouded by a dense fog.

name: Route 211

dialogue.text: The wind howls through here from Mt. Coronet. Hold onto your hat!

mapdesc: A mountain path that tunnels through Mt. Coronet, one of the tallest mountains in Sinnoh.

name: Route 212

dialogue.text: The mansion's Trophy Garden is famous — rare Pokémon appear there!

mapdesc: A tall, sturdy wall surrounds an expansive estate that takes up nearly half of the road space. Beyond the wall, one can see the stately Pokémon Mansion and the owner's prized garden behind it. This path weaves among deep puddles made by the unending rainfall. It is wet and miserable for Trainers. There is a house here whose residents offer to trade TMs for Red, Blue, Yellow, or Green Shards.

name: Route 213

dialogue.text: Hold still! Let me read your Pokémon's footprints… fascinating!

mapdesc: Offshore boulders form a jetty that becalms waves reaching the beach. A resort hotel overlooks the water. In a small house on the beach lives a single man who refers to himself as Dr. Footstep.

name: Route 214

dialogue.text: I sell rare bones from the ruins. Interested in some… archaeology?

mapdesc: The road joining Veilstone City and a lake is described as either "wildly natural" or simply "a mess." There is a cave hewn into the sheer rock wall at the roadside. The hole was made by a Ruin Maniac.

name: Route 215

dialogue.text: It never stops raining on this road. My Pokémon love it, though!

mapdesc: This area is always inundated by heavy rainfall. Only hardy Trainers that can take the rain gather here.

name: Route 216

dialogue.text: The snow never lets up here. There's a lodge ahead — go warm up!

mapdesc: This mountainous road leads from Mt. Coronet. Its constant snowfall and deep snowdrifts impede travelers.

name: Route 217

dialogue.text: This blizzard? This is nothing to the folks of Snowpoint!

mapdesc: Snow blows down from Mt. Coronet and grows into a harsh, ceaseless blizzard. Be prepared for the worst.

name: Route 218

dialogue.text: The fish Pokémon here bite better than anywhere on the coast!

mapdesc: Despite its shortness, this road is revered by fishing enthusiasts as a great, yet little known, fishing spot.

name: Route 219

dialogue.text: The sea south of here goes on and on… all the way to Pal Park!

mapdesc: A beautiful white-sand beach only five seconds on foot from Sandgem Town. A pleasant breeze flows from the sea.

name: Route 220

dialogue.text: Swimming across? Watch for Trainers — they'll battle you mid-stroke!

mapdesc: The sea here is perfect for swimming, with gentle winds and tides. There are sandbars for resting too.

name: Route 221

dialogue.text: That old man at the shrine gives a prize if your Pokémon's level ends in 1!

mapdesc: A straight path hemmed by green grass and sparse stands of trees. At the end of the path stands the gateway to Pal Park.

name: Route 222

dialogue.text: The beach here sparkles! Sunyshore is just up the road!

mapdesc: A sandy beach extends from the road. The beach is busy with avid Fishermen happily casting at the water's edge. A man who loves fishing lives near here. There is also a man who will compare sizes of caught fish.

name: Route 223

dialogue.text: Beyond these waves is the Pokémon League. Almost there, Trainer!

mapdesc: A marine route that requires travelers to navigate around jutting rocks and sandbars.

name: Route 224

dialogue.text: That white rock… I've never seen anything like it. Want to write a message?

mapdesc: With grass fields, rocky outcroppings, the sea, and sandbars, this area is like a miniature of the Sinnoh Region.

name: Route 225

dialogue.text: The Battle Zone's paths are rough and wild — just how I like it!

mapdesc: The path makes its way up and down among rocky outcroppings. It is physically challenging.

name: Route 226

dialogue.text: The currents here are fierce! Only strong swimmers make the crossing.

mapdesc: A path that winds precariously along sheer cliffs that go right to the edge of the sea's pounding waves. There is a small island in the bay. An odd man who speaks foreign languages lives there.

name: Route 227

dialogue.text: That volcano ahead? Stark Mountain. Even the ground here is warm.

mapdesc: A rugged and steep mountain path where vision is limited by steadily falling volcanic ash.

name: Route 228

dialogue.text: This sandstorm never quits! It's the best training weather there is!

mapdesc: This rough path is harshly raked by a horizontally blowing sandstorm driven by strong winds off the sea.

name: Route 229

dialogue.text: Trainers gather in this meadow to battle all day, every day.

mapdesc: A seaside path that makes its way through wildly growing trees and plants.

name: Route 230

dialogue.text: Just a little farther and you're back at the Resort Area. What a swim!

mapdesc: A sea route that stretches from west to east. There is an island that is rich with plant life along the way.

name: New Bark Town

dialogue.text: My research is all about Pokémon evolution! Oh — and could you run an errand for me?

mapdesc: A town where the wind blows and tells of impending change.

name: Cherrygrove City

dialogue.text: I'm the Guide Gent! Follow me — I'll show you everything this town has to offer!

mapdesc: A city where you can smell small flowers and a sea breeze.

name: Violet City

dialogue.text: The Elder of the tower here teaches that all living things are equal.

mapdesc: This old village is still surrounded by trees and other scenery.

name: Azalea Town

dialogue.text: Slowpoke are precious to this town! Whoever's been cutting their tails — unforgivable!

mapdesc: A town where people and Pokémon live together in simple harmony.

name: Goldenrod City

dialogue.text: Biggest city in Johto! The Radio Tower, the Department Store, the Magnet Train — we have it all!

mapdesc: A developing city where people and Pokémon come and go as they like.

name: Ecruteak City

dialogue.text: Two great towers once stood here. One burned to the ground long ago…

mapdesc: A city that even now bears the marks of its history.

name: Olivine City

dialogue.text: Our Gym Leader hasn't left the Lighthouse in days. The Pokémon up there is very sick…

mapdesc: A city where you can hear the melody of the sea.

name: Cianwood City

dialogue.text: My husband trains under the waterfall every single day. Rain or shine!

mapdesc: A beachside city that has benefitted greatly from the sea.

name: Mahogany Town

dialogue.text: Try our famous RageCandyBar! …No, this shop is not a hideout. Why do you ask?

mapdesc: A town with a suspicious air to it. It's a hiding place for ninjas.

name: Blackthorn City

dialogue.text: The den behind this city is sacred. Only those the Elder approves may enter.

mapdesc: A mysterious mountain village cut into a rock face.

name: Dark Cave

dialogue.text: It's pitch black in here! Without Flash I can't see my own Pokémon!

mapdesc: A dark, difficult-to-navigate tunnel that runs beneath the Johto region.

name: Sprout Tower

dialogue.text: The great pillar sways but never breaks — like a Bellsprout's stalk.

mapdesc: A tower where they study diligently to learn to live with Pokémon.

name: Ruins of Alph

dialogue.text: These carvings on the walls… they look like they're watching us, don't they?

mapdesc: A place where you can find a former adventure.

name: Union Cave

dialogue.text: This cave links three roads. Easy to get in — hard to pick your way out!

mapdesc: Deep underground, it's connected to the ocean. The water flows here.

name: Slowpoke Well

dialogue.text: They were down this well, cutting off Slowpoke Tails to sell. The nerve!

mapdesc: When Slowpoke are thirsty, they come here to drink.

name: Ilex Forest

dialogue.text: The forest's protector rests in that tiny shrine. Show it respect.

mapdesc: A large forest full of trees that are used to make charcoal.

name: National Park

dialogue.text: I'm going to win the Bug-Catching Contest this week! Me and my Scyther!

mapdesc: A spacious and beautiful park. It's connected to the Pokéathlon Dome.

name: Pokéathlon Dome

dialogue.text: Jump! Dash! Throw! Here, your Pokémon's athletic dreams come true!

mapdesc: A great dome standing west of the National Park, where Trainers and their Pokémon compete in athletic events.

name: Burned Tower

dialogue.text: Ten years I've chased the beast that fled this tower. TEN years!

mapdesc: A tower that was hit by lightning, and subsequently caught fire and burned.

name: Bell Tower

dialogue.text: A rainbow-colored Pokémon once perched at the very top of this tower.

mapdesc: A tower that's said to have a that lands there.

name: Glitter Lighthouse

dialogue.text: Amphy's light guides every ship home to Olivine. Climb up and say hello!

mapdesc: It shines brightly over the ocean with the power of Ampharos.

name: Cliff Cave

dialogue.text: These cliffs stack three layers high. Only real climbers pass through here.

mapdesc: A cave partway up the sheer cliffs between the Safari Zone and Route 47.

dialogue.text: Welcome to the brand-new Safari Zone! You can even rearrange the areas!

name: Mt. Mortar

dialogue.text: A karate master trains alone somewhere deep inside this mountain.

mapdesc: A naturally large cavern that is incredibly spacious.

name: Lake of Rage

dialogue.text: A RED Gyarados?! In this lake?! Grab your rod, kid!

mapdesc: A huge lake full of clear, blue water.

name: Ice Path

dialogue.text: The ice in here never melts. One wrong step and you slide all the way back!

mapdesc: A cave that connects Route 44 to Blackthorn City.

name: Whirl Islands

dialogue.text: The whirlpools guard something — a Pokémon they call the god of the sea.

mapdesc: Four islands that are protected by whirlpools and are difficult to reach.

dialogue.text: A tower, embedded right into the cliff face… Who could have built such a thing?
You stand in sacred ground. Answer truly: what do Pokémon mean to you?

name: Route 29

dialogue.text: You can't catch Pokémon without Poké Balls! Trust me, I tried.

mapdesc: A road that begins a journey. The road smells like freshly cut grass.

name: Route 30

dialogue.text: My Rattata is in the top percentage of all Rattata!

mapdesc: A grassy path where you can battle other young Trainers.

name: Route 31

dialogue.text: The tower in the city ahead? The Sages there test your bond with Pokémon.

mapdesc: A short, nostalgic path winding through nature.

name: Route 32

dialogue.text: Longest route in Johto, this one. The Pokémon Center is still a ways off…

mapdesc: A path crossed by the Magnet Train bridge--nature meets technology.

name: Route 33

dialogue.text: It's always drizzling on this little road. My Pokémon love the mud!

mapdesc: A difficult and always rainy path that comes out in a cave.

name: Route 34

dialogue.text: The Day Care is just up the road — my grandpa runs it with grandma!

mapdesc: A lush, green path, even though it's right next to a city.

name: Route 35

dialogue.text: The gate ahead leads into the park. No littering, no running, no Weedle!

mapdesc: A bright path with a lake that's easy on the eyes and easy to traverse.

name: Route 36

dialogue.text: A weird tree blocked this road for YEARS. Turns out it danced when you watered it!

mapdesc: A green and densely overgrown path that forks in two directions.

name: Route 37

dialogue.text: On stormy nights, they say three legendary beasts race past this very spot.

mapdesc: A popular route for Trainers, marked by three Apricorn trees.

name: Route 38

dialogue.text: Strong trainers line this road — everyone's toughening up before Olivine.

mapdesc: A path that weaves through trees and comes out at a farm.

name: Route 39

dialogue.text: Our poor Miltank stopped giving milk… She just needs Oran Berries and love.

mapdesc: A downhill path that passes through a grassy and breezy plain.

name: Route 40

dialogue.text: The sea is calm today — perfect for the long swim out to the islands!

mapdesc: You can hear the waves on this path from west Olivine City to the sea.

name: Route 41

dialogue.text: Feel that pull? The whirlpools ahead will spit you right back out!

mapdesc: A rough spot in the sea where whirlpools block your path.

name: Route 42

dialogue.text: A mountain splits this route clean in half. Flying over beats climbing through!

mapdesc: A small path opened at the base of challenging Mt. Mortar with multiple entrances to it.

name: Route 43

dialogue.text: Those gate guards charging a toll? They're not even real guards!

mapdesc: A woodland path that comes out at a lake. Beware the greedy gate.

name: Route 44

dialogue.text: Weather changes fast up here. The pond's full of Poliwag, though!

mapdesc: A wonderful path with a few pleasant springs and abundant greenery.

name: Route 45

dialogue.text: This mountain road only goes DOWN. Pick your path before you hop!

mapdesc: A winding road by a river, but called "Mountain Road."

name: Route 46

dialogue.text: See those ledges? You can hop down, but there's no climbing back up!

mapdesc: A way home that runs from the base of the mountains to the plains.

name: Route 47

dialogue.text: Cliffs above, sea caves below. This road stacks three layers high!

mapdesc: A road on a cliff that strikes fear in the hearts of those who dare to pass.

name: Route 48

dialogue.text: The grass out here grows taller than me! Wild Pokémon love it.

mapdesc: A pleasant path from the base of the mountain to the Safari Zone.

name: Pallet Town

dialogue.text: A world-famous Pokémon Lab, in our sleepy little town. Imagine that!

mapdesc: A fairly new and quiet town. It's a small and pretty place.

name: Viridian City

dialogue.text: Our Gym stood empty for ages. The new Leader is from Pallet Town, they say.

mapdesc: A beautiful city that is enveloped in green year-round.

name: Pewter City

dialogue.text: The museum here keeps fossils of Pokémon from ancient times.

mapdesc: A quiet city nestled between rugged mountains and rocks.

name: Cerulean City

dialogue.text: A mermaid? Oh — you mean our Gym Leader. She swims like one!

slogan: A Mysterious Blue Aura Surrounds It
mapdesc: A beautiful city with flowing water and blooming flowers.

name: Vermilion City

dialogue.text: The S.S. Aqua sails from this port all the way to Olivine. All aboard!

mapdesc: A southern city that is bathed in orange by the setting sun.

name: Lavender Town

dialogue.text: The old tower is a Radio Tower now. The departed rest in the House of Memories.

mapdesc: A small town covered in a beautiful hue of purple.

name: Celadon City

dialogue.text: The Department Store has EVERYTHING. My wallet never stood a chance!

mapdesc: A rich, rainbow colored city where people and Pokémon gather.

name: Saffron City

dialogue.text: Silph Co. towers over everything here. And our Gym Leader bends spoons with her mind.

mapdesc: The biggest city in Kanto, shining with a golden light.

name: Fuchsia City

dialogue.text: The old Safari Zone is a preserve now. The Warden still misses the old days.

mapdesc: A historic village that has become new.

name: Cinnabar Island

dialogue.text: The volcano took the whole town… Only the Pokémon Center still stands.

slogan: The Fiery Town of Burning Desire
mapdesc: A town used to be here until it was swept away by an eruption.

name: Indigo Plateau

dialogue.text: Beyond this hall wait the Elite Four. Champions only past this point!

mapdesc: The fate of many Trainers aiming for the top rests here.

name: Power Plant

dialogue.text: We're back online! This plant powers the Magnet Train itself.

mapdesc: The plant that generates all the energy for the Kanto region.

name: Rock Tunnel

dialogue.text: No lights in this tunnel — none! Without Flash it's a maze in the dark.

mapdesc: A dark tunnel that begins in Lavender Town and comes out in Cerulean City.

name: Pal Park

dialogue.text: Pokémon from faraway lands settle here. The park welcomes them all.

mapdesc: This used to be the location of the Safari Zone, but is now the Pal Park.

name: Diglett's Cave

dialogue.text: Diglett dug this whole tunnel?! All the way to the other side of the mountains?!

mapdesc: A tunnel dug by Diglett connecting Vermilion City to Pewter City.

name: Mt. Moon

dialogue.text: On clear nights, Clefairy gather here to dance around the Moon Stone.

mapdesc: A mountain where a star fell. It now holds a mysterious power.

name: Viridian Forest

dialogue.text: A natural maze full of Bug Pokémon! I could stay here forever!

mapdesc: A forest filled with nature and Bug-type Pokémon.

name: Seafoam Islands

dialogue.text: Twin islands, mirrored across the strait — with icy caverns underneath.

mapdesc: Two small islands lightly floating in the ocean.

name: Cerulean Cave

dialogue.text: Only proven masters may enter. Something impossibly strong lives in there.

mapdesc: A cave that had collapsed once. It has been reconstructed.

name: Tohjo Falls

dialogue.text: These falls mark the border — one region behind you, another ahead.

mapdesc: The Tohjo waterfall that links the Kanto and Johto regions.

name: Mt. Silver

dialogue.text: Even the League fears this mountain. A lone trainer waits at the peak, they say…

mapdesc: A hallowed mountain that rises between the Johto and Kanto regions.

name: Victory Road

dialogue.text: So quiet in here now… everyone takes the waterfall shortcut these days.

mapdesc: A road that tests Trainers aiming at the Pokémon League.

name: Route 1

dialogue.text: Fresh country air! This little road runs straight between two towns.

mapdesc: A country road full of greenery and rough paths.

name: Route 2

dialogue.text: The forest gate is just ahead — or cut through the Diglett tunnel if you're rushed!

mapdesc: A path that winds and bends from the forest's entrance.

name: Route 3

dialogue.text: Everyone on this path is itching for a battle. The mountain's just ahead!

mapdesc: A road where many rocks have fallen from the sky to create craters.

name: Route 4

dialogue.text: The mountain tunnel spits you out here. It's all downhill to the city!

mapdesc: A one-way road down a hill that has a gentle slope and is fun to traverse.

name: Route 5

dialogue.text: The Underground Path is closed off… you'll have to go through the big city's gate.

mapdesc: A road running north of Saffron City connecting to the Underground Path.

name: Route 6

dialogue.text: The port city is just south — you can smell the sea from here!

mapdesc: A road running south of Saffron City connecting to the Underground Path.

name: Route 7

dialogue.text: The big city's just west. I swear I can smell the Department Store perfume!

mapdesc: A short road that leads to the blocked-off Underground Path.

name: Route 8

dialogue.text: The gatehouse ahead used to demand a drink before letting anyone through. A DRINK!

mapdesc: A short road that leads to the blocked-off Underground Path.

name: Route 9

dialogue.text: Rocky path ahead — it dead-ends at a tunnel. Bring something that glows!

mapdesc: A road that forms a maze crossing a small, rocky mountain.

name: Route 10

dialogue.text: Hear that hum across the water? That's the Power Plant working again.

mapdesc: A road that passes along the canal and comes out at the Power Plant.

name: Route 11

dialogue.text: A giant sleeping Pokémon used to block this road. Snored like thunder!

mapdesc: A grassy path with a gentle, refreshing breeze.

name: Route 12

dialogue.text: This long wooden bridge is a fishing paradise. Quiet — they're biting!

mapdesc: A bridge known for its fishing. It's also called "Silence Bridge."

name: Route 13

dialogue.text: These hedges twist into a maze! I've been lost for an hour…

mapdesc: A difficult, narrow path where many Trainers await you.

name: Route 14

dialogue.text: The flocks fly low over the tall grass here. Perfect for training my birds!

mapdesc: A pleasant coastal road where the breeze blows and waves roar.

name: Route 15

dialogue.text: Almost there! The sea wind carries the smell of the preserve up this road.

mapdesc: A path that cuts through the row of trees to come out on the coastline.

name: Route 16

dialogue.text: Cycling Road starts here — it's downhill the whole way! WHEEE!

mapdesc: The northern starting point of Cycling Road.

name: Route 17

dialogue.text: Me and my bike are one on this slope. Try to keep up!

mapdesc: An easy path of Cycling Road running above the sea.

name: Route 18

dialogue.text: End of the Cycling Road! My legs are jelly, but what a ride.

mapdesc: The southern ending point of Cycling Road.

name: Route 19

dialogue.text: Straight south through open water — twin islands split the sea ahead.

mapdesc: A coastal road littered with rocks due to Cinnabar Island's volcano eruption.

name: Route 20

dialogue.text: The current between the islands is fierce. Strong swimmers only!

mapdesc: A popular path with swimmers. The Seafoam Islands are also here.

name: Route 21

dialogue.text: From the water here you can see the little town with the famous Lab!

mapdesc: A coastal road where the only threat might be the volcano on the horizon.

name: Route 22

dialogue.text: The road to the League gate starts here. Rivals like to ambush challengers…

mapdesc: A path to Victory Road that eventually becomes impassable.

name: Route 23

dialogue.text: Only trainers with every badge make it this far up the mountain road.

mapdesc: A mountain path where only the strongest Trainers may pass.

name: Route 24

dialogue.text: This is Nugget Bridge! Beat five trainers in a row and win a golden prize!

mapdesc: A beautiful bridge that stretches across a river.

name: Route 25

dialogue.text: The famous inventor's Sea Cottage sits at the very end of this path!

mapdesc: A path that winds through the forest and comes out overlooking the sea.

name: Route 26

dialogue.text: The League gate ahead checks for ALL sixteen badges. SIXTEEN!

mapdesc: An unimaginably difficult mountain road that gives the impression of a test.

name: Route 27

dialogue.text: Across the water behind you is a whole other region. This is where it begins!

mapdesc: A road that crosses from Johto to the Kanto region, like a new journey.

name: Route 28

dialogue.text: The trail to the silver peak… Few climb it. Fewer come back down.

mapdesc: A vacant and hidden mountain road that continues to Mt. Silver.

dialogue.text: This wreck ran aground years ago. Divers say there's still treasure in the flooded rooms.
An old tomb in the trees. The braille inside speaks of a titan of steel.
Something slumbers behind these sandstone walls — a titan of rock, the legend says.
A passage under the desert… fossils of Pokémon long gone are buried down here.

name: Dewford Town

dialogue.text: Trends are everything on this island! Have you heard the latest? It's SO now.

slogan: A tiny island in the blue sea.
mapdesc: New trends are always the rage among the inhabitants of this small island town.

name: Ever Grande City

dialogue.text: All that stands between you and the League now is Victory Road. Good luck, Trainer.

slogan: The paradise of flowers, the sea, and Pokémon.
mapdesc: This city is blanketed in a profusion of colorful blooms. It plays host to the grand Pokémon League.

name: Fallarbor Town

dialogue.text: Ash keeps falling from Mt. Chimney… but the soil grows the best Berries because of it.

slogan: A farm community with small gardens.
mapdesc: A town formed by scholars who gather to research meteorites.

name: Fiery Path

dialogue.text: This tunnel cuts straight through the mountain's fire. Watch where you step!

mapdesc: The Pokémon that live in this area like to use the steam that erupts from the ground as a sort of bath.

name: Fortree City

dialogue.text: Every house here is built up in the treetops! Careful crossing the bridges.

slogan: The treetop city that frolics with nature.
mapdesc: The people and the Pokémon of this city follow nature's cues to rise each morning and end each day.

name: Granite Cave

dialogue.text: There are wall paintings deep inside! The old man in the entrance can tell you about them.

mapdesc: This cavern is famous for its cave art, which depicts events from thousands of years ago.

name: Battle Frontier

dialogue.text: Seven facilities, seven Frontier Brains. This island is the ultimate test!

slogan: The front lines of Pokémon battling!

name: Route 101

dialogue.text: This is where the Professor got attacked by a wild Pokémon! You should grab a partner.

mapdesc: This grassy path running between Littleroot Town and Oldale Town is perfect for doing fieldwork.

name: Route 102

dialogue.text: The grass here is full of Berries to pick and little Pokémon to catch!

mapdesc: Many Trainers gather on this route in the hope of encountering Pokémon in the wild.

name: Route 103

dialogue.text: Cross the water and you can battle your rival by the shore.

mapdesc: On weekends and holidays, fishing enthusiasts flock to every section of coastline on this seaside route.

name: Route 104

dialogue.text: The beach on one end, the flower shop on the other, and a forest between!

mapdesc: This path, rich with water and colorful plant life, runs north and south of Petalburg Woods.

name: Route 105

dialogue.text: Watch the tides out here — the little islands go under when the water rises.

mapdesc: This water route boasts gentle currents, which makes it safe for even poor swimmers to bask in.

name: Route 106

dialogue.text: Granite Cave's just up the beach. The Gym Leader trains in there sometimes.

mapdesc: This is the site of serious battles between Fishermen of the seaside area and the wild Pokémon of the seas.

name: Route 107

dialogue.text: Nothing but open sea between the islands here. Great fishing if you Surf out.

mapdesc: The local children of Dewford Town practice long-distance swimming in the waters of this aquatic route.

name: Route 108

dialogue.text: There's a rusted old ship anchored out here. Divers love poking around it.

mapdesc: People come swimming from as far away as the Kalos region to see the site of Sea Mauville.

name: Route 109

dialogue.text: Best beach in Hoenn! Watch the little kids building sandcastles.

mapdesc: People and Pokémon alike enjoy ocean swims in this water route.

name: Route 110

dialogue.text: Cycling Road runs right over this route — and the trick house is hidden nearby!

mapdesc: A timeworn path where nature remains untouched. Above it on this route stretches the huge Cycling Road.

name: Route 111

dialogue.text: The sandstorm never quits in the desert stretch. You'll need special goggles to enter.

mapdesc: This expansive route includes a desert where sandstorms rage unceasingly and a mountain pass.

name: Route 112

dialogue.text: The cable car station is right here — it hauls you up to Mt. Chimney's peak.

mapdesc: This route is popular among Trainers because it offers the chance to stroll while gazing up at Mt. Chimney.

name: Route 113

dialogue.text: It's not snow — it's volcanic ash! Collect it and the glassworker will make you something.

mapdesc: The tall grass on this route is painted a dusty gray by the volcanic ash that pours from Mt. Chimney.

name: Route 114

dialogue.text: My brother the Fossil Maniac dug his whole house into the hill over there.

mapdesc: This mountain path to Meteor Falls is so long and arduous that even Hikers have difficulty tackling it.

name: Route 115

dialogue.text: The wind off the sea is fierce here. Good training weather, if you can stand up in it!

mapdesc: This trail to Rustboro City was broken by people who lived at Meteor Falls long ago.

name: Route 116

dialogue.text: This road runs to the tunnel where they're digging — carefully — around the Whismur.

mapdesc: A path that many workers take on their daily commute between Rustboro City and Rusturf Tunnel.

name: Route 117

dialogue.text: The Pokémon Day Care is right here. Leave two and who knows — you might get an Egg!

mapdesc: A path where many Trainers gather to raise their Pokémon and train them for battle.

name: Route 118

dialogue.text: A wide river cuts this route in two. You'll need to Surf, or catch a ride across.

mapdesc: This seaside route brings together the east and west sides of the Hoenn region.

name: Route 119

dialogue.text: It rains without stopping here. The Weather Institute studies it — and a rare Pokémon lives up top.

mapdesc: If you are not prepared for it, the harsh conditions of this tropical rain forest will defeat you in less than five minutes.

name: Route 120

dialogue.text: The bridge is long and the rain is thick. Something invisible brushed past me…

mapdesc: Pokémon that can camouflage themselves hide in the rich wilds along this route.

name: Route 121

dialogue.text: Team's up to something on Mt. Pyre, just south of here. Gives me the creeps.

mapdesc: This road leads to both the Safari Zone and Mt. Pyre. Many people set out along this route from Lilycove City.

name: Route 122

dialogue.text: Mt. Pyre rises right out of the sea here. Best pay your respects before you pass.

mapdesc: People make their way to Mt. Pyre over this water route, reliving many precious memories as they traverse it.

name: Route 123

dialogue.text: There's a Berry garden along this route. The old couple will teach you all about growing them.

mapdesc: This route offers a convenient path back to Mauville City from Mt. Pyre, but be aware that it is a one-way road.

name: Route 124

dialogue.text: Divers go down all over this bay hunting for the treasure on the seafloor.

mapdesc: This great stretch of ocean connects the cities of Lilycove, Mossdeep, and Sootopolis.

name: Route 125

dialogue.text: Shoal Cave's on the island out here. Bring shells to the old man for a prize!

mapdesc: Small children are allowed to play in the waters of this sea route as far as Shoal Cave—but no further!

name: Route 126

dialogue.text: The town under the sea is near here — Sootopolis, in its crater. You have to Dive to reach it.

mapdesc: Even a pro swimmer would need three entire days to circuit around the crater containing Sootopolis City.

name: Route 127

dialogue.text: Deep water all around. Perfect for Surfing races — and for the big biters lurking below.

mapdesc: With no place to stop and rest in these waters, it is very hard for a swimmer to make it across this route.

name: Route 128

dialogue.text: The sea churns here… I heard the two ancient titans clashed somewhere close by.

mapdesc: The ocean floor beneath this water route is rumored to contain an undiscovered ruin.

name: Route 129

dialogue.text: Whirlpools and deep water. The waves have been strange ever since the weather turned.

mapdesc: Many Pokémon Trainers visit this route to train their Pokémon before challenging the Pokémon League.

name: Route 130

dialogue.text: There's a mirage island out here that only appears some days. My grandpa's chased it for years!

mapdesc: This route was once a big topic of conversation due to a strange island that seemed to appear and then disappear.

name: Route 131

dialogue.text: The sky-tower rises from an island near here. They say a legendary dragon sleeps at its peak.

mapdesc: It is the custom in Pacifidlog Town to swim a lap around this route before breakfast.

name: Route 132

dialogue.text: The currents shove you around out here — you can only go the way the water lets you.

mapdesc: The children of Pacifidlog Town are said to be such strong swimmers that they frolic and play in these fierce currents.

name: Route 133

dialogue.text: Almost to the far island! The current finally works with you on this stretch.

mapdesc: Trainers gather in this stretch of sea in search of something beyond its fierce currents.

name: Route 134

dialogue.text: Last leg of the crossing. Ride the strong current straight west and don't fight it!

mapdesc: This route is rather a famous diving spot among those who love exploring ancient ruins.

dialogue.text: No regular Poké Balls in here! Use the Safari Balls and don't scare them off.

name: Victory Road

dialogue.text: The final cave before the League. Every Trainer here has all eight badges — like you.

mapdesc: The great cave beneath Ever Grande City — the final test of Trainers bound for the Pokémon League.

dialogue.text: The braille on the wall speaks of a titan of ice sealed away long ago.

name: Jagged Pass

dialogue.text: Mind the slope — it's all loose ash and switchbacks down to Lavaridge.

mapdesc: This mountain route is the result of a volcanic eruption in ages long past. It can only be traversed on an Acro Bike.

name: Lavaridge Town

dialogue.text: Soak in the hot springs a while! The sand bath'll do your Pokémon good, too.

mapdesc: A popular spot in the Hoenn region, thanks to its hot springs, said to cure any ailment.

name: Lilycove City

dialogue.text: The Department Store here is the biggest in Hoenn. And the Contest Hall's next door!

slogan: Where the land ends and the sea begins.
mapdesc: This tourist destination is undergoing a revival, thanks to the popularity of its Pokémon Contest Spectaculars.

name: Littleroot Town

dialogue.text: This town is so small there's not even one road out of it… you have to cut through the tall grass!

slogan: A town that can't be shaded any hue.
mapdesc: Basking amid vibrant nature, this simple town is not shaded with any one hue.

name: Mauville City

dialogue.text: Right in the middle of Hoenn! The Bike Shop owner will even give you a free bike.

slogan: The bright and shiny city of fun!
mapdesc: This large city is located in the heart of the Hoenn region, at the crossroads of its nostalgic past and new technology.

name: Meteor Falls

dialogue.text: A meteorite fell here ages ago. The falls run deep — deep enough for a Waterfall.

mapdesc: This waterfall is said to have been the site of a meteor shower. An ancient people once made their home here.

name: Mirage Island

dialogue.text: Some days the island is just… there, shimmering over the water. Then it's gone.

mapdesc: An island on Route 130 that only appears on rare days, shimmering above the waves.

name: Mossdeep City

dialogue.text: The Space Center launches real rockets! Our Gym Leaders are twins, you know.

mapdesc: Research is underway day and night in this city, all in the hope of understanding the distant reaches of space.

name: Mt. Chimney

dialogue.text: The cable car takes you right to the crater's rim. Feel that heat!

mapdesc: This volcano towers to a height of nearly . The view from its peak is beyond compare.

name: Mt. Pyre

dialogue.text: The departed rest at the summit. Two orbs were once kept here — red and blue.

mapdesc: The mountain is where spirits of Pokémon have rested and will always rest: past, present, and future.

name: New Mauville

dialogue.text: A generator underground went haywire — the whole facility hums with loose Voltorb!

mapdesc: Though it was planned to be a subterranean city extending 69 floors underground, this project died in development.

name: Oldale Town

dialogue.text: See how the two shops have different-colored roofs? That's how you tell a Mart from a Center!

mapdesc: A town where the contrast between colorful flowers and deep, verdant forests is most beautiful.

name: Pacifidlog Town

dialogue.text: Our town floats on rafts lashed together! When the tide's right, the currents change.

slogan: Where the morning sun smiles upon the waters.
mapdesc: This town first came into being as a floating storehouse used by people living on the ocean's surface.

name: Petalburg City

dialogue.text: The Gym Leader here is my papa. He won't battle me until I've proven myself…

mapdesc: A whiff of salt is always in the air in this city, which is skirted by the ocean shore.

name: Petalburg Woods

dialogue.text: Watch out for that shady man in the woods — he's from some team or another.

mapdesc: This dense forest, with its abundance of trees, is well known to be a favorite habitat for Shroomish.

name: Rustboro City

dialogue.text: This is Hoenn's biggest city! The Devon company and the Trainers' School are both here.

slogan: The city probing the integration of nature and science.
mapdesc: This city is the main hub of industry in the Hoenn region, with the Devon Corporation as its beating heart.

name: Rusturf Tunnel

dialogue.text: They stopped digging this tunnel so the Whismur wouldn't be disturbed. Shh!

mapdesc: This stone tunnel links together Rustboro and Verdanturf. Its name was chosen as a mixture of the two.

name: Scorched Slab

dialogue.text: A small cave behind the waterfall. The rock inside is scorched black.

mapdesc: A small cave hidden behind the waterfall on Route 120, its rock scorched black.

name: Seafloor Cavern

dialogue.text: Deep under the sea… this is where they woke the slumbering titan.

mapdesc: It is said that once dwelled here in the ancient mists of a time long past.

name: Sealed Chamber

dialogue.text: You have to Dive to even find this place. The walls are covered in braille…

mapdesc: An undersea chamber on Route 134, reachable only by Dive, its walls covered in ancient braille.

name: Shoal Cave

dialogue.text: The cave floods and empties with the tide! Come back at low tide for the shells.

mapdesc: This cave's appearance changes greatly between high tide and low tide.

name: Sky Pillar

dialogue.text: The tower where the sky dragon sleeps. Only the Mach Bike can cross that crumbling floor.

mapdesc: This tower was created for the veneration of a that lives far above in the heavens.

name: Slateport City

dialogue.text: The market by the beach sells everything! And the Oceanic Museum's just up the way.

slogan: The port where people and Pokémon cross paths with nature.
mapdesc: People from many different regions gather and mingle in this bustling port city.

name: Sootopolis City

dialogue.text: This whole town sits inside a crater! You can only reach it by diving or flying.

slogan: The mystical city where history slumbers.
mapdesc: This city, which rises from the crater of a great meteoroid crash, can only be reached through the sea or from the sky.

dialogue.text: For a world covered by the sea! That's Team Aqua's dream, land-dweller!
Welcome to my Trick House! Solve my puzzle — if you can find me first. Heheh!

name: Verdanturf Town

dialogue.text: The air here is so clean it's famous for it! Perfect for folks who need to rest.

slogan: The windswept highlands with the sweet fragrance of grass.
mapdesc: Thanks to the prevailing wind pattern, this town is always kept clear of falling volcanic ash.

dialogue.text: We study Hoenn's rain here — some of the heaviest in the world falls just outside.

name: Abundant Shrine

dialogue.text: People come to this shrine to wish for their Pokémon's health. The little ones gather here.

mapdesc: People once lived in this village, and it still feels the same as it did then.

name: Accumula Town

dialogue.text: Did you catch that speech in the plaza? 'Liberate your Pokémon,' they said. Gave me chills.

mapdesc: This town offers great views due to its many hills.

name: Anville Town

dialogue.text: The trains all come here for repairs. On weekends collectors show up hunting for rare items.

mapdesc: A city with a rail yard where trains park for an overhaul.

name: Aspertia City

dialogue.text: There's a lookout up on the hill with the best view in Unova. The new Gym's right here, too.

slogan: A city that reaches for the sky.
mapdesc: A city at the foot of high mountains where you can see all of Unova.

name: Castelia City

dialogue.text: Biggest city in Unova! Skyscrapers everywhere, and the best ice cream you'll ever have.

mapdesc: A big city with skyscrapers piercing the clouds.

name: Castelia Sewers

dialogue.text: Watch your step down here — grunts in funny outfits have been skulking around the tunnels.

mapdesc: Iron bars around the exits were dismantled, drawing curious Trainers.

name: Cave of Being

dialogue.text: Three little guardians of the lakes were born in a place like this, the old stories say.

mapdesc: They say the deepest part of the cave leads to the Sinnoh region.

name: Celestial Tower

dialogue.text: Ring the bell at the top for the Pokémon laid to rest here. Its tone tells you their spirit.

mapdesc: A tall tower with a large bell whose tones are said to purify the spirit.

name: Challengers Cave

dialogue.text: Only strong Trainers bother with this cave. That's exactly why I train in here!

mapdesc: A cave in western Unova that only opens to Trainers who have entered the Hall of Fame — packed with tough battlers.

name: Chargestone Cave

dialogue.text: The floating stones are all charged with electricity! They move when you push through them.

mapdesc: A cave where electrically charged stones float.

name: Clay Tunnel

dialogue.text: The Gym Leader had this tunnel dug himself. It runs clear under the city to the old ruins.

mapdesc: A tunnel created by Clay's constant mining. It leads to Twist Mountain.

dialogue.text: A refrigerated warehouse full of crates — and the Pokémon that snuck in from the cold.

name: Desert Resort

dialogue.text: A whole desert in the middle of Unova! The sandstorm hides old ruins near the center.

mapdesc: It is a popular place for tourists, but too harsh to be a resort.

name: Dragonspiral Tower

dialogue.text: Oldest structure in Unova. They say the original dragon slept coiled inside for centuries.

mapdesc: The oldest tower in the Unova region. No one knows its history.

name: Dreamyard

dialogue.text: Kids play in these ruins now, but the Musharna here can pull dreams right out of the air.

mapdesc: A plant site used as a playground for children and Pokémon.

name: Driftveil City

dialogue.text: It's a rough-and-ready market town by the drawbridge. The Cold Storage is just outside.

mapdesc: A port town distributing many goods, and a gateway to the Unova region.

name: Driftveil Drawbridge

dialogue.text: The longest drawbridge in Unova! When it lifts for the ships, feathers drift down onto the deck.

mapdesc: A drawbridge raises and lowers, depending on the ship schedules.

name: Floccesy Ranch

dialogue.text: The ranch dog — er, Herdier — keeps the whole herd in line. One of ours went missing today, though.

mapdesc: Before long, the area became a ranch as Pokémon and people gathered.

name: Floccesy Town

dialogue.text: Quiet farming town. My grandpa runs the ranch just up Route 20 — go say hi!

mapdesc: The town is famous for a clock tower that tells of the town's beginnings.

name: Giant Chasm

dialogue.text: A crater blasted out of the forest. It's bitterly cold at the bottom — and something powerful lives there.

mapdesc: Legend says that if you approach this big chasm, disasters surely follow.

name: Humilau City

dialogue.text: A resort city built on the water! Everyone gets around by Surfing between the boardwalks.

mapdesc: A resort city where buildings float in the waves and look like islands.

name: Icirrus City

dialogue.text: It snows most of the year here. Careful in the marsh outside — the fog gets thick.

slogan: Sky Glittering with Flowers of Snow
mapdesc: In winter, the city is covered with snow as far as the eye can see.

name: Lacunosa Town

dialogue.text: Everyone locks their doors at night here. Old legend about a beast that came from the sky…

mapdesc: A town where all honor old customs, living as methodically as clockwork.

name: Lentimas Town

dialogue.text: Tiny town on the mountainside. The volcano and the old strange house loom right over us.

mapdesc: The town boasts of porcelain and pottery made from volcanic clay.

name: Liberty Garden

dialogue.text: There's a lighthouse on this little island. A rare event Pokémon was sighted here once!

mapdesc: An island symbol of hope for a world where humans and Pokémon live free.

name: Lostlorn Forest

dialogue.text: A traveling battle troupe camps in this little wood. And rare Pokémon slip in from far away.

mapdesc: It was once known as a place where people got lost for no reason.

name: Marine Tube

dialogue.text: A glass tunnel right along the sea floor! You can watch the water Pokémon swim overhead.

mapdesc: An undersea tunnel built using cutting-edge technology.

name: Mistralton Cave

dialogue.text: There's a guidance chamber deep inside where one of the guardian dragons is said to rest.

mapdesc: A forgotten cave sealed with hints of a legend's presence.

name: Mistralton City

dialogue.text: Cargo planes take off from the runway here. The Gym's in an airplane hangar — full of cannons!

mapdesc: Vegetables are grown by the runway and transported by cargo plane.

name: Moor of Icirrus

dialogue.text: The whole marsh floods in the wet season. Great fishing — if you don't mind sinking in the mud!

mapdesc: They say water collecting in hollows on the flat land created the moor.
imageCredit: Bulbapedia (Moor of Icirrus Keldeo event BW.png)

name: Nacrene City

dialogue.text: The Gym's inside a museum, and the whole city used to be a warehouse district. Very artsy now.

mapdesc: A city established in restored storehouses built 100 years ago.

name: Nimbasa City

dialogue.text: Roller coasters, a ferris wheel, two stadiums — this is the entertainment city!

slogan: Lit by the Flash of Lightning!
mapdesc: A bustling city of entertainment, with many theme parks.

name: N's Castle

dialogue.text: The whole castle erupted up out of the ground beneath the League. Nobody saw it coming.

mapdesc: It once appeared in the past as a base for Team Plasma...

name: Nuvema Town

dialogue.text: The Professor's lab is right here. She studies where Pokémon come from — the big questions!

mapdesc: A rural town whose sea breezes give the sense of something coming.

name: Opelucid City

dialogue.text: The Dragon-type Gym is here. The whole city looks different depending on which version you're in!

mapdesc: A convenient city of rapid change, showing no traces of the past.

name: P2 Laboratory

dialogue.text: An abandoned lab out on the water. Whatever they were building here, they left in a hurry.

mapdesc: Team Plasma's secret lab is located on a quiet little island.

name: Pinwheel Forest

dialogue.text: Two ways through: the straight path, or the deep woods full of big old trees and bugs.

mapdesc: You can go through hollows of fallen trees, too.

name: Relic Castle

dialogue.text: This buried castle sinks down through the sand. Deeper than anyone's ever gone, they say.

mapdesc: Ancient ruins with a glorious history, buried in the sand as time went by.

name: Relic Passage

dialogue.text: This long underground passage connects the city to the desert ruins. Watch for lurking grunts.

mapdesc: A recently discovered passage that leads to the Relic Castle.

name: Reversal Mountain

dialogue.text: One side blazing, the other freezing — the whole mountain's split in two climates.

mapdesc: A volcano that grabs headlines for its many recent eruptions.

name: Seaside Cave

dialogue.text: The tide pools freeze the floor into slides. Push the ice blocks to make your way across.

mapdesc: A rock that became passable due to wind, waves, and Pokémon.

name: Skyarrow Bridge

dialogue.text: Longest bridge in the region — it soars from the forest gate straight into the big city.

mapdesc: The biggest, longest bridge in Unova has four supporting towers.

name: Strange House

dialogue.text: The furniture moves on its own and the stairs don't go where they should. I'm not staying the night!

mapdesc: A house known for a sad incident that is said to keep people away.

name: Striaton City

dialogue.text: The three brothers run both the restaurant AND the Gym. Which one you battle depends on your starter!

mapdesc: Entry stairs built in memory of a former home in a snowier climate.

name: Tubeline Bridge

dialogue.text: Trains rumble underneath while we walk across the top. Bikers love bombing down this one.

mapdesc: A sturdy steel bridge that won't budge an inch when trains cross it.

name: Twist Mountain

dialogue.text: The tunnels twist and climb every which way. Miners and Hikers both get lost in here.

mapdesc: It has a reputation as a mine littered with valuable ore.

name: Undella Bay

dialogue.text: Best diving in Unova! Divers hunt for treasure on the seabed all summer long.

mapdesc: A world-famous sea of shining waves.

name: Undella Town

dialogue.text: Summer resort town! A famous Champion keeps a villa here and visits every season.

mapdesc: A summer retreat with a beach full of people who enjoy summer vacations.

name: Pokémon League

dialogue.text: The League sits at the top of Victory Road. Beat the Elite Four and the Champion waits beyond.

mapdesc: Only Trainers who win at all the Pokémon Gyms may challenge it.

name: Route 1

dialogue.text: First route out of Nuvema. Just tall grass and little Patrat as far as the eye can see.

mapdesc: A small road by the shore, where you can enjoy the seascape in peace.

name: Route 10

dialogue.text: Last road before Victory Road. The League checkpoint turns back anyone missing a badge.

mapdesc: A road leading to the Badge Check Gates, chock full of showoffs.

name: Route 11

dialogue.text: A bridge road east of the dragon city. Big bug Pokémon love the trees along the rails.

mapdesc: Limpid streams carved beautiful scenery on this road.

name: Route 12

dialogue.text: The road runs down to the sea, past a little wood. Feels like the far edge of the region out here.

mapdesc: A place of fun, where it's enjoyable just walking over its gentle hills.

name: Route 13

dialogue.text: A coastal cliff road with a hidden hermit's house tucked into the rocks below.

mapdesc: This seaside route is famous for sandbars that cross the ocean.

name: Route 14

dialogue.text: The road winds down to a quiet beach and a wishing shrine in the trees. Peaceful spot.

mapdesc: A road covered with mist from the s.

name: Route 15

dialogue.text: There's an abandoned resort just off the road here. Strong Trainers gather in its ruined rooms.

mapdesc: A road whose sharp cliffs may scare some people off.

name: Route 16

dialogue.text: Short green road on the edge of the big city. The forest troupe used to camp nearby.

mapdesc: Many who visit Nimbasa City drop by to take a break.

name: Route 17

dialogue.text: It's all whitewater rapids out here! You ride the current down on a raft — hold on tight!

mapdesc: The fast current here makes it difficult to .

name: Route 18

dialogue.text: A rocky little island route at the mouth of the rapids. A rare event Pokémon turned up here once.

mapdesc: Some researchers believe it was once contiguous with Desert Resort.

name: Route 19

dialogue.text: First route out of the new town in the southwest. Wild Pokémon in every patch of grass!

mapdesc: A path formed at the foot of mountains. It has a gradual slope.

name: Route 2

dialogue.text: Straight shot north to the science city. Plenty of Trainers itching to battle along the way.

mapdesc: A pastoral road where novice s challenge one another.

name: Route 20

dialogue.text: The road to the ranch, split by a little river. Lots of families out for a stroll.

mapdesc: In , fallen leaves accumulate and hide the ground beneath.

name: Route 21

dialogue.text: You can only reach this stretch by Surfing. Quiet water, big fish, and not another soul around.

mapdesc: Many rocks make swimming difficult. Proud s come to show off.

name: Route 22

dialogue.text: A short road running up toward the harbor town. The nightclub's thumping bass carries all the way out here.

mapdesc: A lot of ups and downs and ledges make the route a natural maze.

name: Route 23

dialogue.text: The final road before the League checkpoint. Only the toughest Trainers linger up here.

mapdesc: A mountain path of no return with overwhelming cliffs.

name: Route 3

dialogue.text: There's a school bus stuck on this road and a little pond off to the side. Preschoolers everywhere!

mapdesc: A long winding road with lots of ponds and tall grass.

name: Route 4

dialogue.text: Half-finished buildings and blowing sand — this road runs right alongside the big city.

mapdesc: A delayed the road construction to connect two cities.

name: Route 5

dialogue.text: Street performers line the road into the amusement city. Somebody's always looking for a battle.

mapdesc: A busy road where performers gather.

name: Route 6

dialogue.text: It follows the river up toward the cave. The rangers here keep an eye on the wild Deerling.

mapdesc: A road with many trees for nature lovers.

name: Route 7

dialogue.text: A long ridge road with an old house at the end. They say it's haunted — I believe it.

mapdesc: Raised walkways help you avoid the tall grass.

name: Route 8

dialogue.text: The road runs on a boardwalk over the marsh. Mud everywhere off the path — stay on the planks!

mapdesc: It rains a lot here, and the marshy swamp holds many .

name: Route 9

dialogue.text: There's a whole shopping mall built right on this route! Best place to stock up before the League road.

mapdesc: This paved road attracts those who love bikes.

name: Victory Road

dialogue.text: The last road to the League winds up through the cliffs. Only Trainers with eight badges may climb.

mapdesc: The craggy final ascent to the Pokémon League, laced with caves and cliffside switchbacks.

name: Village Bridge

dialogue.text: Folks actually live in little shops built right on the bridge! Someone's always singing out here.

mapdesc: An old bridge that settlers of the Unova region built and now live on.

name: Virbank City

dialogue.text: Smoggy little harbor town. The Gym's in a nightclub — the Pokéstar Studios are next door.

slogan: City of falling fog and rising stars!
mapdesc: A city often covered by clouds and smoke, with very active residents.

name: Virbank Complex

dialogue.text: Overgrown lots behind the factories. Wild Pokémon and tough Trainers both love the tall grass here.

mapdesc: A complex that is designed so Pokémon can work there easily.

name: Wellspring Cave

dialogue.text: The tunnel runs deeper than anyone's mapped. You hear water everywhere but can't see it.

mapdesc: The underwater pool grew from rainwater soaking into the ground.

name: Ambrette Town

dialogue.text: The Fossil Lab is here! They can bring Pokémon back from ancient stones.

mapdesc: This town was known only for its aquarium until the discovery of rare Fossils really put it on the map.

name: Anistar City

dialogue.text: The great pink sundial by the sea… they say it came from space, and glows at dusk.

slogan: The starry city marking the hours.
mapdesc: Some say the enigmatic device used as a sundial came from outer space.

name: Aquacorde Town

dialogue.text: Everyone meets at the café tables by the river. That's where journeys start around here!

mapdesc: A town that naturally sprang up as people flocked to this pristine riverside.

name: Azure Bay

dialogue.text: Islands, sea grass, and the old Sea Spirit's Den out in the middle. Great Surfing!

mapdesc: The deep blue ocean off Kalos's coast, where Pokémon from distant lands are said to visit.

name: Camphrier Town

dialogue.text: An old, old town under an old, old castle. The Shabboneau family fell on hard times…

slogan: A town where you can taste the ages.
mapdesc: This ancient town was once famous for the long-neglected manor home of a noble family.

name: Chamber of Emptiness

dialogue.text: A bare stone chamber with nothing inside. Why does it feel like something's watching?

mapdesc: A mysterious void. It is said that nothing can exist within it.

name: Connecting Cave

dialogue.text: They call it Zubat Cave for a reason. Bring earplugs!

mapdesc: This cave linking Route 7 and Cyllage City is notable for its great hordes of Zubat.

name: Coumarine City

dialogue.text: Half port, half hillside — ride the monorail between them! The Gym's up in a great tree.

slogan: The city where bay and butte are bound.
mapdesc: An exclusive resort area made popular thanks to its clear skies and mild atmosphere.

name: Couriway Town

dialogue.text: Waterfalls on every side! The train from the far region used to stop at this station.

mapdesc: The railway brings people from great distances to see the huge, majestic falls.

name: Cyllage City

dialogue.text: Beach on one side, cliffs for climbing on the other. Our Gym is a rock wall you scale!

mapdesc: A city nestled between the cliffs and the sea, overlooked by steep Bicycle racecourses.

name: Dendemille Town

dialogue.text: A little farming town between the mountains. The windmill keeps turning through the snow.

slogan: A town where fluff dances on the wind.
mapdesc: A rural town where Pokémon and windmills work together to farm the land in a chilly latitude.

name: Frost Cavern

dialogue.text: The deeper you climb, the colder it gets. An abominable Pokémon guards the heart of it.

mapdesc: The drifting snow and impenetrable fog make this cavern a place of fantastical illusions.

name: Geosenge Town

dialogue.text: The strange stones standing around town? Nobody knows who placed them. Or why.

mapdesc: A town lined with mysterious stones and encircled by strange ruins of old.

name: Glittering Cave

dialogue.text: The crystals down here glow on their own — and fossils sleep in the walls.

mapdesc: Lose all sense of direction as you wander though this cave, where Luminous Moss glows emerald green.

name: Pokémon League

dialogue.text: A castle of four chambers — Fire, Water, Steel, Dragon — and the Champion's hall above.

mapdesc: This path is open only to those who can best every Pokémon Gym in the Kalos region.

name: Power Plant

dialogue.text: The badlands plant powers Lumiose's tower. Strange folk in red suits were sniffing around…

mapdesc: An environmentally responsible power plant that generates solar power from space.

name: Route 1

dialogue.text: The shortest route in the world! Just a garden path between two towns, really.

mapdesc: A small and quiet country lane that connects Vaniville Town and Aquacorde Town.

name: Route 10

dialogue.text: Rows of strange standing stones line this road… like a field of monuments.

mapdesc: Countless stones line this mysterious path in such numbers and arrays that it seems overwhelming.

name: Route 11

dialogue.text: Crystals bloom right out of the hillside here, all the way to the mirrored cave.

mapdesc: One can feel the power of the earth's interior from the crystals that sprout along this mountain path.

name: Route 12

dialogue.text: The seaside ranch road! Skiddo graze free — you can even ride them.

mapdesc: Frolicking Skiddo can be seen and ridden at the Baa de Mer Ranch, located beside the breezy sea.

name: Route 13

dialogue.text: Wind turbines tower over the badlands. The power plant hides in the dunes.

mapdesc: Since no plants can grow in the red clay of this great plain, it hosts the Power Plant instead.

name: Route 14

dialogue.text: A swamp of dead trees and deep bog. The scarecrows… some of them move.

mapdesc: The lush trees and boggy swamps of this trail give off an eerie vibe, even in broad daylight.

name: Route 15

dialogue.text: Autumn leaves and fallen logs — the ruined hotel squats at the road's end.

mapdesc: This path has become a popular hangout for the wild and directionless youths of Lumiose City.

name: Route 16

dialogue.text: The mushroom marsh road! Locals harvest them by the basket.

mapdesc: This rustic path beside the glittering metropolis of Lumiose City is a well-known fishing spot.

name: Route 17

dialogue.text: Snow too deep to walk — a Mamoswine carries you across the drifts!

mapdesc: Due to constant snowstorms and heavy snowfall, humans have no hope of traversing this road on foot.

name: Route 18

dialogue.text: The old mining valley. Rails and carts everywhere, and the cave mouth gaping above.

mapdesc: This path is best known for its trolley, once used for the coal mine, and the curious Inverse Battle house.

name: Route 19

dialogue.text: The great bridge over the marsh — you can see half of southern Kalos from the middle.

mapdesc: This great valley can now be crossed thanks to its long bridge, built with the help of many Pokémon.

name: Route 2

dialogue.text: Your first tall grass! The Professor's friends will show you how catching works.

mapdesc: You'll find many Pokémon hiding among the tall grass that grows in tufts along this trail.

name: Route 20

dialogue.text: The winding woods play tricks — paths that loop, clearings that move.

mapdesc: This path was designed to disturb the woods as little as possible, so it twists and turns among the trees.

name: Route 21

dialogue.text: The last stretch before Victory Road, half river, half ruin.

mapdesc: This is the path of the chosen, designed to hone the stalwart Trainers who dare face the Pokémon League.

name: Route 22

dialogue.text: Détourner Way loops behind the badlands — a shortcut for those who know it.

mapdesc: This lively path bustles with many Trainers and their Pokémon, all gathered here to train themselves.

name: Route 3

dialogue.text: Ponds, songbirds, and a gentle road into the forest. Perfect first stroll.

mapdesc: The little rises and hollows of this lush forest are a favorite place for many kinds of Pokémon to play.

name: Route 4

dialogue.text: A parterre garden all the way to Lumiose — hedges, roses, and fountain mazes!

mapdesc: This famed path's perfectly designed and executed gardens are its highlight and a special point of pride.

name: Route 5

dialogue.text: Roller skaters own this road! Grind the rails down toward the old town.

mapdesc: Roller Skaters from across the Kalos region gather on this hilly path to demonstrate their best skills.

name: Route 6

dialogue.text: The tree-lined drive to Parfum Palace. Fancy folk, fancier Pokémon.

mapdesc: This tree-lined path was once covered with grass as tall as a man, but it was cleared by the palace.

name: Route 7

dialogue.text: The riverside road past the Day Care — and a very large Pokémon asleep on the bridge!

mapdesc: The longest single road in the Kalos region runs straight alongside one of its greatest rivers.

name: Route 8

dialogue.text: The cliff road forks: down to the fossil beach, or along the bluffs to the sea.

mapdesc: This is a road of great contrasts, from the harsh rock of the cliffs to the soft sands of the beach.

name: Route 9

dialogue.text: Too rocky to walk — you cross the badlands on a rented Rhyhorn!

mapdesc: This rocky passage was created by retired Rhyhorn racers who forged a path where there was none.

name: Victory Road

dialogue.text: The final gauntlet — ruins, caves, and cliffs stacked between you and the League.

mapdesc: This treacherous path to the Pokémon League will break the hearts of even the toughest Trainers.

name: Kiloude City

dialogue.text: You can only reach this southern city by TMV! The Battle Maison never closes.

slogan: A kind, sweet, and exquisite city.
mapdesc: This city, popular with Pokémon Trainers, is a typical example of southern Kalosian life.

name: Laverre City

dialogue.text: Our Gym is inside a dollhouse of a tree, and our Leader is a famous designer!

mapdesc: An unearthly city created by those inspired by a mysterious and ancient tree 1,500 years old.

name: Lost Hotel

dialogue.text: A grand hotel, ruined and dark. The punks who squat there won't say what happened.

mapdesc: This once-famous hotel clings to the shade of its former glory after tragedy left it in ruins.

name: Lumiose City

dialogue.text: The City of Light! Two hundred streets, five Pokémon Centers, and the Prism Tower Gym!

mapdesc: A dazzling metropolis of art and artifice, located in the very heart of the Kalos region.

name: Parfum Palace

dialogue.text: Admission is 1,000 Poké! The chandeliers alone are worth a fortune, mon ami.

mapdesc: A luxurious palace constructed 300 years ago by a king who wished to display his power to all.

name: Poké Ball Factory

dialogue.text: Every Poké Ball in Kalos rolls off this line! …Why is the conveyor running backward?

mapdesc: An expansive factory where every Poké Ball used in the Kalos region is produced.

name: Pokémon Village

dialogue.text: Deep past the forest, mistreated Pokémon live free. A very wise one watches over them.

mapdesc: Legends say a place exists where Pokémon live in hiding, but no one has ever found it.

name: Reflection Cave

dialogue.text: The mirrored walls show a dozen of you. Some say the reflections aren't all yours…

mapdesc: The sheer faces of these rock walls reflect the images of people and Pokémon like nature's mirror.

name: Santalune City

dialogue.text: The Gym here is inside a giant spider's web — the Leader photographs Bug Pokémon!

mapdesc: Many beginning Trainers gather in this friendly city to start a Pokémon journey.

name: Santalune Forest

dialogue.text: This forest feels just like one in a faraway region… Bug Pokémon love it here.

mapdesc: The gentle light filtering through this sun-dappled forest makes it a popular spot for nature walks.

name: Sea Spirit's Den

dialogue.text: A tiny cave in the bay where the sea spirit is said to rest. Fishermen leave it offerings.

mapdesc: A pit in the shallows of Azure Bay thought to have been from an ancient outpouring of energy.

name: Shalour City

dialogue.text: The Tower of Mastery rises from the bay — the secret of Mega Evolution sleeps inside.

mapdesc: The seaside home of the Tower of Mastery, where the legend of mysterious stones lives on.

name: Snowbelle City

dialogue.text: It never stops snowing here. The Gym is a palace of ice — dress warm!

mapdesc: They say the cold air flowing from the Pokémon Gym is responsible for this city's frozen state.

name: Terminus Cave

dialogue.text: Miners abandoned these tunnels. They say a legendary serpent coils in the deepest pit.

mapdesc: A coal mine that was closed a few years ago due to rumors of a monster living deep within it.

name: Vaniville Town

dialogue.text: Your new neighbors just moved in! The Professor already sent word about you kids.

slogan: A town whose flower is about to bloom.
mapdesc: Blooming buds covered in morning dew exude hope for the future in this small town.
